topic: An employee finds out a junior colleague makes more money
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the office hummed with an irritating constancy. I was reviewing the quarterly budget reports, a task that usually brought a dull sense of routine, when I stumbled across Sarah’s salary information on the shared HR portal. My eyes scanned the figures, then immediately darted back to Sarah's name. A slow, disbelieving smile spread across my face, quickly morphing into a slack-jawed stare. I reread the numbers twice, my fingers hovering over the mouse as if expecting the screen to change. It was a significant discrepancy. I felt a sudden, strange lightness in my chest, a feeling like someone had just unexpectedly lifted a heavy weight. When Sarah finally walked by, her cheerful greeting sounded distant, and I could only manage a weak nod, my gaze fixed somewhere beyond her shoulder.

[story 2]

I leaned back in my ergonomic chair, the leather squeaking under my weight. The data pop-up had been undeniable. The figures for the junior analyst, Mark, were markedly higher than mine, even factoring in his shorter tenure. I blinked several times, trying to recalibrate what I was seeing. My jaw tightened almost involuntarily, and I gripped the edge of my desk until my knuckles went white. I let out a breath I hadn't realized I was holding, the air feeling suddenly thin in my lungs. I looked down at my keyboard, the keys suddenly feeling alien and unresponsive. The world seemed to have tilted a fraction of a degree; everything looked sharper, more defined, and utterly rearranged.

[story 3]

It happened during a casual check of the departmental payroll spreadsheet. I was cross-referencing some expense reports when I landed on the comparison chart. The number associated with the newest hire, Chloe, dwarfed mine by a considerable margin. I froze, my hand hovering over the mouse. My breath hitched, a sharp, shallow intake of air that seemed to catch in my throat. I slowly pulled my hand back from the screen, my eyes wide and unfocused, staring blankly at the beige wall opposite my monitor. A strange tremor ran through my hands, and I felt a distinct urge to look away, to retreat entirely from the reality of the numbers presented before me.
